project_overview.project_name: Djibouti Agri-Food Value Chain Development Project (FAR)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve the competitiveness and the resilience of selected agri-food value chains in the areas targeted by the project
project_overview.total_financing: US$17.8 million (IDA US$15 million, Government US$1 million, Beneficiaries US$1.8 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Rural and peri-urban households involved in agri-food sector, producers, agri-food entrepreneurs, women and youth (estimated 1,500 direct beneficiaries)

primary_locations: Northern Zone: Tadjourah and Obock regions (Awdaa plain)
Southern Zone: Dikhil and Ali Sabieh regions (Mouloud-Grand Bara-Hol Hol)
Central Zone: Arta region and Djibouti city peri-urban area (PK25 Barrage de l'Amitié)
geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with focus on three growth poles
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: no - Djibouti is classified as a small state but not fragile or conflict-affected

key_components.component_name: Public infrastructure and services for agri-food value chain development
key_components.description: Climate-proof public infrastructure (boreholes, solar pumping, rural roads, land preparation), regulatory framework strengthening, innovation support including demonstration greenhouse

key_components.component_name: Support to agri-food investment and access to financing
key_components.description: Business plan development support, advisory services to financial institutions, Capital Endowment Contributions to agri-food investors
Project Implementation Unit operations, monitoring and evaluation, grievance redress mechanism
Swift response mechanism for eligible crises or emergencies
key_components.financing: US$0 million (funded through reallocation if needed)

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Agriculture, Water, Fisheries and Livestock, in charge of Halieutic Resources (MAEPE-RH)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Three-tiered structure: Project Implementation Unit (PIU), Project Steering Committee (PSC), Sub-Project Selection Committee (SPSC)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Standard Procurement Documents, mostly national market procurement, simplified procedures for sub-project beneficiaries
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PIU-based M&E system with dedicated specialist, semester data collection, baseline and follow-up surveys, Mid-Term Review

identified_risks.risk_description: Direct support to private entrepreneurs is departure from past policies; requires technological leap forward
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Technical assistance for bankable business plans; promotion of innovations and climate-smart technologies
Institutional support through specialized TA services and strong PIU staffing

identified_risks.risk_description: Complex contracts including infrastructure works and technical assistance; limited FM capacity
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong PIU expertise, dedicated financial officer, external auditor, customized financial software

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak organization of value chains; possible reluctance of financial institutions to lend
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Regulatory framework improvements; TA assistance to commercial banks for agri-food sector knowledge

identified_risks.risk_description: Insufficient interest and capacity of investors to prepare bankable sub-projects
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: TA support to agri-food entrepreneurs for solid bankable sub-projects preparation

identified_risks.risk_description: Country's macro political situation and governance outlook
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Considered satisfactory and should not impact negatively on project implementation

identified_risks.risk_description: Civil works risks, water/energy consumption, food safety, land management, pesticide use
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental and Social Management Framework (ESMF), screening procedures, mitigation measures, grievance mechanism

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - effective GRM with capacity to receive and respond to grievances within 4-week period
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Special attention to women agri-food investors with 10% higher CEC grants, priority in training and selection, assistance with land use authorization

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project does not operate in conflict-affected areas; Djibouti has stable political situation
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Djibouti classified as small state but not fragile; rural poverty rates much higher than urban (45% vs 13.6%); climate vulnerability and food insecurity challenges
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Presence of multiple foreign military bases provides stability; no security risks identified for project implementation
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - project includes support for vulnerable groups including refugees as potential beneficiaries with preferential treatment
